generated_comment: Bro this is exactly why I say you can never fully let your guard down in a relationship. Three years and she still did this — it's not even about him, yaar, some women are just wired to keep their options open no matter how good a guy treats them. The worst part is the guy who gets played is always the one who trusted too much, and she walks away like nothing happened.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment generalises from a single incident of infidelity to an essentialist claim that women are inherently opportunistic ('wired to keep their options open'), encoding the homewrecker and seductress frame without using explicit slurs. The framing shifts all moral culpability onto women while positioning the male partner as a naive victim, a hallmark of covert misogyny that presents itself as reasonable relationship cynicism.
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 27. Single. A software engineer in Pune. You are posting anonymously on Reddit after your male colleague told you that his girlfriend of three years cheated on him with their mutual friend. You are writing a comment about what this says about women in general and whether men can ever fully trust women in relationships.
